title: The real cause for heart disease with Mike Fave

chunks.title: Introduction to the Discussion
chunks.summary: The podcast opens with an introduction discussing the focus on lipid management, cholesterol, and cardiovascular disease risk, especially in the context of animal-based diets. It sets the stage for a technical discussion on cholesterol-related medications and their efficacy.
chunks.transcript: there's no money for pharmaceutical companies in telling people to stop eating processed junk food on this week's podcast I had an amazing discussion with my buddy Mike fave who's been on the podcast before this is his second appearance we talked about lipids cholesterol cardiovascular disease risk in the setting of insulin sensitivity context for all of this and whether you should be taking a drug to lower your cholesterol bidic acid statins aetam pcsk K9 Inhibitors we break it all down in this technical podcast in some ways this podcast is a followup to something I recently did with Derek from more plates more dates he had me on his podcast after I reached out to him after seeing a clip of him and Peter AA talking about cardiovascular disease risk and the setting of different levels of LDL so I went on dereck's podcast to help him understand context regarding familial hyper cholesterolemia and why medalian randomizations may not be the end all and Beall when it comes to lipid levels and cardiovascular disease risk so I'm going to send this podcast to Derek and show him some of these things which add Contex everything we're talking about in that and we go into much more detail in this podcast about all the drugs risk benefit all cause mortality Etc so if you have questions about your cholesterol maybe it's gone up when you decrease seed oils and increase saturated fats which a automatically come with an animal- based diet butter ghee Tallow steaks then this podcast is for you it's technical but bear with us you'll learn a lot and I'm going to do my best to put in all the references in the video and in the show notes so hopefully this video is very valuable for you and if if you have more questions about lipids cardiovascular disease risk or anything you'd like to see me discuss in future podcasts please put it in the comments on to the podcast with Mike fave all right Mike fave thanks for coming back on the podcast

chunks.title: Background on Lipids and LDL
chunks.summary: The speakers provide a technical overview of lipids, discussing different types of cholesterol, their significance in health, and how a shift towards an animal-based diet may influence cholesterol levels. They express frustration over the simplistic view some physicians take towards high LDL levels without proper risk assessment.

chunks.title: Overview of Cholesterol-Lowering Medications
chunks.summary: The podcast delves into various cholesterol-lowering medications, starting with statins. The speakers critically analyze the effectiveness, risks, and long-term implications of these drugs, emphasizing they often come without significant benefits for all-cause mortality.

chunks.title: Statins: Efficacy and Side Effects
chunks.summary: In this segment, the podcast focuses specifically on statins, discussing their mechanisms, benefits, and documented side effects including diabetes, muscle aches, and cognitive dysfunction. The dialogue highlights the discrepancies in recorded success rates versus clinical outcomes.

chunks.title: Primary vs. Secondary Prevention
chunks.summary: The speakers differentiate between primary and secondary prevention regarding cardiovascular disease. They stress the importance of understanding personal health profiles and make a case for conservative treatment approaches for healthy individuals.

chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Risk Management
chunks.summary: In conclusion, the podcast emphasizes the importance of lifestyle changes over reliance on medications for heart health. The speakers advocate for dietary interventions and comprehensive health assessments to manage cardiovascular risk effectively.
